title: Protect yourself from immigration fraud - Canada.ca
content:
## Immigration and citizenship fraud and scams
Learn how to protect yourself from scammers and what to do if you are a victim of fraud.
- Find out if your representative is authorized
- Check if your school is on the designated learning institutions list
- Find answers to common questions about immigration fraud and scams

## Services and information

### Online and telephone scams
Identify artificial intelligence, social media, website, email and phone scams targeting applicants

### Fraud
                        targeting newcomers
Learn how to avoid and report scams in Canada.

### International
                        student scams
Learn how to protect yourself from fraud while applying for a study permit.

### How
                        to report fraud or abuse
Help protect yourself and others by reporting immigration fraud.

### Consequences
                        of fraud
Submitting false documents, lying on an application or cheating on a citizenship test has serious
                    consequences.

### Marriage
                        fraud
Avoid scams and learn the rules for sponsoring a spouse.

### Routine
                        surveys
Find out what surveys are currently being conducted by IRCC.

### Posters
                        and tip sheets
View and download materials to help you identify common scams.

### Using
                        an immigration and citizenship representative
Find out how to use a representative to help with your immigration or citizenship application.

### Check
                                if your representative is authorized
If you’re using a paid immigration representative (consultant, lawyer or Quebec notary), they
                            must be licensed to represent you or give advice.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
